issuesFound.title: Page title is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. Users relying on screen readers or browser tabs cannot identify the page content or purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <nav> elements on the page. This instance (id="main-nav") lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 44: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element for the hero dashboard lacks an alt attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the image content to users who cannot see it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 148:

issuesFound.title: Link has non-descriptive text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Learn more" does not describe the destination. Users navigating by links cannot distinguish this from other "Learn more" links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 190: Learn more about the scanner

issuesFound.title: Button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button contains only a span with aria-hidden="true". It has no visible text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby, so screen readers announce it as "button" with no name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 45: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element "Feature" lacks a scope attribute. In a table with multiple header columns, scope="col" is required to define the header axis for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 364: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy jumps from <h2> (Line 221) to <h5> (Line 234). Intermediate levels (h3, h4) are skipped, which disrupts the document outline for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 234:

Connect your project

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The section uses aria-labelledby="features-heading", but no element with id="features-heading" exists in the document. The section will have no accessible name.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 179:

Everything you need to ship accessible software

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button controls expandable content (indicated by aria-controls="faq-2-answer") but lacks aria-expanded. Screen readers cannot announce whether the content is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 496: <button type="button" aria-controls="faq-2-answer" aria-expanded="false" id="faq-2-question">

issuesFound.title: Form input missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has no associated <label>, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Screen readers cannot identify the purpose of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 583: <input type="email" id="newsletter-email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true" placeholder="you@example.com" aria-label="Email address for newsletter">

issuesFound.title: Personal data input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The input collects personal data (first name) but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents browsers from assisting users in filling out the form.
issuesFound.fix: Line 616: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, but outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring when outline is none. Keyboard users will not see any focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 408: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors media query
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), :focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. In high-contrast mode, this removes the native focus indicator without providing a visible replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 419: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link's :focus-visible state sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Keyboard users navigating to the logo will not see any focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 668: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button's :focus-visible removes outline with only outline-offset: 3px. No visible focus indicator remains for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 697: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set outline: none with only outline-offset: -3px and border-radius. The negative offset does not create a visible focus ring when outline is none.
issuesFound.fix: Line 739: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input's :focus-visible state sets outline: none with outline-offset: 0. While the :focus state has a box-shadow, :focus-visible does not inherit it, leaving keyboard focus invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 777: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button's :focus-visible removes outline with only outline-offset: 3px. No visible focus indicator is provided for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 789: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. Border-radius alone does not create a visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 807: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. Keyboard users will not see which link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 962: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. No visible focus indicator remains for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1088: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Keyboard users cannot see which billing option is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1178: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Keyboard users cannot see when this region has focus for scrolling.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1263: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons set outline: none with only outline-offset: -3px. The negative offset does not create a visible focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1402: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs' :focus-visible state sets outline: none with outline-offset: 0. The :focus state has box-shadow but :focus-visible does not, leaving keyboard focus invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1439: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Keyboard users cannot see which checkbox has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1449: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. No visible focus indicator is provided.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1467: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. Keyboard focus is not visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1475: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. No visible focus indicator remains.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1483: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 630: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px, min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons need adequate touch target size for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 651: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px, height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are sized at 20px × 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate small checkboxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1446: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto instead of position: absolute with 1px dimensions and clip rect. Content marked as screen-reader-only will be fully visible on screen, defeating the purpose of the utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 453: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with transition: transform, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 508 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 508: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg) with transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 929 has empty rule bodies that do not disable the transform.
issuesFound.fix: Line 929: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses words together and impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to parse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 354: body { word-spacing: 0em; letter-spacing: 0em; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which compresses letters together and impairs readability. This can particularly affect users with dyslexia or low vision who need clear letter separation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355: body { letter-spacing: 0em; }

issuesFound.title: No forced-colors/high-contrast-mode override for interactive elements
issuesFound.explanation: Button classes (.btn, .btn-primary, .btn-secondary, .btn-outline, .btn-ghost, .btn-sm, .btn-lg, .btn-icon) set colour and background-color but the @media (forced-colors: active) block only covers :focus-visible and .skip-link. Interactive elements may become invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 578: (fix requires adding forced-colors overrides for all button classes in the @media (forced-colors: active) block)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links. While some links gain underlines on hover, navigation links (#nav-menu > li > a, nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a) only change colour on hover, violating SC 1.4.1 Use of Colour.
issuesFound.fix: Line 330: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; }

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the error message is linked via aria-describedby, but aria-invalid is never set to true on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Line inlineValidator._renderResult (valid branch): insert `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` immediately after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`
  Line inlineValidator._renderResult (invalid branch): insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown and hidden via CSS classes and the hidden attribute, yet the associated trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never toggled. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded or collapsed state of the submenu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Billing-period buttons behave as a single-select toggle group, but their active state is communicated only through CSS. Each button needs aria-pressed so assistive technologies can announce which billing period is selected.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is not set to true (the close function does set it to false). This leaves screen-reader users unaware that the answer panel has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible page content, yet no aria-live announcement is made. Screen-reader users are not informed that a menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open: insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` before the closing brace.
  Line mobileNav.close: insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates price values across all plan cards, but the new prices are not announced. Screen-reader users may not realize the content has changed.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox is shown and hidden dynamically, but the combobox input’s aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the open or closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line siteSearch._open: insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of the function.
  Line siteSearch._close: insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus open and close without an accompanying live-region announcement. Users navigating with a screen reader receive no feedback when a submenu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: insert `liveRegion.announce('Submenu opened');` before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: insert `liveRegion.announce('Submenu closed');` before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons indicate the active category only via a CSS class. Because they behave as mutually exclusive toggle buttons, they must expose their pressed state via aria-pressed so screen-reader users know which filter is active.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ panels expand and collapse without live-region feedback. Although the functions accept an announceToSR parameter, they never use it to trigger an announcement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line faqAccordion.open: insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) || item.question + ' expanded');` before `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', ...);`
  Line faqAccordion.close: insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) || item.question + ' collapsed');` before `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter grid update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Changing filters updates the visible product cards, count badge, and empty-state message, but no aria-live region announces the new result count or empty state. Screen-reader users remain unaware that the grid has changed.

issuesFound.title: productFilter view-mode change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Toggling between grid and list view changes the layout visually, but no live-region message informs screen-reader users of the new view mode.
issuesFound.fix:
Line productFilter._onViewToggle: after `_productFilterState.viewMode = mode;`, insert:
  ```javascript
  liveRegion.announce(`Products displayed in ${mode} view.`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded and aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile-nav trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" or aria-controls pointing to the drawer on page load. Assistive technologies cannot infer that the button controls a hidden panel.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown trigger is registered, it receives aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialised to false even though the panel starts hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu.register: after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, insert:
  ```javascript
  setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
  ```

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input starts with its suggestion listbox hidden, but aria-expanded is not initialised to false on the input element.
issuesFound.fix:
Line siteSearch.init: after `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-describedby', srUtils.ensureId(_statusEl));`, insert:
  ```javascript
  setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
  ```

issuesFound.title: productFilter category change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a category filter tab changes the visible product grid, but no live-region message announces the new category or result count.

issuesFound.title: productFilter sort change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the sort order rearranges the product grid without notifying screen-reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line productFilter._onSortChange: after `applyFilters();`, insert:
  ```javascript
  liveRegion.announce(`Products sorted by ${_productFilterState.sortKey}, ${_productFilterState.sortDir === 'asc' ? 'ascending' : 'descending'}.`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: productFilter text filter not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Typing in the text filter hides and shows product cards dynamically, but no live-region announcement informs screen-reader users of the updated result count.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Removing an active filter chip updates the product grid, but the removal action and new result count are not announced.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button triggers lack keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers that are not native button or summary elements are given role="button" and tabindex="0", but the container keydown handler only supports arrow navigation. It does not handle Enter or Space, so keyboard users cannot activate these triggers.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: The close function accepts a returnFocus parameter but never uses it. When a dropdown closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button, leaving keyboard users stranded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`, insert:
  ```javascript
  if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });
  ```

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing-period toggle is given role="switch", but its checked state

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button (`.nav-toggle`) is toggled by `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions, but neither function updates the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger to reflect the open/closed state. Additionally, `mobileNav.init()` does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not announce this state change to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1679: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
  Line 1696: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `._closeInstance()` functions change the visible state of dropdown panels without announcing the change to screen readers. Users are not notified when a menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1843: liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');
  Line 1853: liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button (`item.trigger`). The `close()` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where screen readers may not recognize the item as expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3638: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion triggers missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` and `close()` functions toggle the visibility of FAQ answers without announcing the change to screen readers. Users are not informed when an answer is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3638: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) || 'Answer expanded');
  Line 3665: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) || 'Answer collapsed');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch._open()` and `._close()` functions show and hide the suggestion listbox but do not update `aria-expanded` on the search input (`_inputEl`). The `init()` function also fails to initialize `aria-expanded="false"`. Screen reader users cannot tell if suggestions are visible.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle missing aria-checked update
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._syncToggleState()` function updates the visual state of the billing period toggle (`_toggleEl`) by toggling a CSS class, but it does not update the `aria-checked` attribute. Since the element has `role="switch"`, `aria-checked` is required to convey the state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3253: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for period change
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod()` function updates prices and UI labels when the billing period changes, but it does not announce this change to screen readers. Users are not informed that prices have switched between monthly and annual billing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3238: liveRegion.announce(period === 'annual' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: productFilter module missing live region announcement for count change
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters()` function updates the product count badge and visibility of cards, but it does not announce the new count or filter application to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match the selected filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2853: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || 'Products filtered');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` function marks a field as invalid by adding an error description but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element. Screen readers rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid fails to clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid()` function clears the error description but does not remove the `aria-invalid` attribute or set it to "false". The field may remain announced as invalid to screen readers even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1396: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure toggle button for navigation sub-menus controls the visibility of the submenu via the isOpen state but does not expose this state to assistive technology. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button triggering the mobile navigation submenu sections uses the isExpanded state to show/hide content but does not pass aria-expanded to the button. This hides the state change from screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 675: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons track the activeFilter state to visually indicate selection but do not use aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify which category is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1263: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle Monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing button reflects the selected value state visually but lacks aria-pressed. Assistive technology users are not informed which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1530: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle Annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button reflects the selected value state visually but lacks aria-pressed. Assistive technology users are not informed which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1537: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel uses role="dialog" to indicate modality but is missing aria-modal="true". This fails to inform assistive technology that content outside the dialog is inert.
issuesFound.fix: Line 655: <div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>

issuesFound.title: Button spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner span rendered inside the Button component during loading is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". This may cause screen readers to announce irrelevant content or noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line 905: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk indicating a required field is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce "asterisk" unnecessarily if the field is already marked as required programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 990: *

issuesFound.title: HeroSection missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major landmark region but lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish this section from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1085: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Although it contains a visually hidden heading, the section element itself is not programmatically labelled for landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1165: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element containing skip links lacks an aria-label. If multiple nav elements exist on the page, users cannot distinguish this one from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 835: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The article element for each pricing plan lacks an aria-labelledby reference to its heading. This prevents screen reader users from identifying the card's purpose when navigating by landmarks or articles.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1585: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className={['pricing-card', plan.featured ? 'pricing-card--featured' : '', isSelected ? 'pricing-card--selected' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page (item.current) lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed which link represents the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 565: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: StarRating individual stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star spans inside the StarRating component are decorative but lack aria-hidden="true". Since the container has an aria-label, the individual icons should be hidden to prevent redundancy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button enters a loading state (isSubmitting) but does not use aria-busy. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is temporarily unavailable due to processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1895: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button enters a loading state (isSubmitting) but does not use aria-busy. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is temporarily unavailable due to processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2065: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing aria-label with item name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA link in the pricing card lacks an aria-label that includes the plan name. While it has visible text, unique aria-labels ensure clarity when links are listed out of context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1625: <a href={plan.ctaHref} className={['btn', plan.featured ? 'btn--primary' : 'btn--outline', 'btn--md',].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} for ${plan.name} plan`}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integration logos used for visual looping is not hidden from assistive technology. This causes screen readers to read the same list of integrations twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1225: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

fpTitles: PlanCard CTA link missing aria-label with item name
